strengths: Hands-on distributed systems depth validated at scale — Sociomantic role involved low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second, directly relevant to real-time inference and event streaming requirements.
Active AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks, demonstrating current, practical exposure to the tooling domain.

Broad cross-stack ownership and a consistent pattern of picking up unfamiliar systems quickly (e.g., took over iOS development mid-project, built GIS platform independently), suggesting high adaptability.
concerns: No demonstrated engineering leadership at any meaningful scale — the entire career arc is individual contributor and staff-level; there is no evidence of building, structuring, or scaling an engineering organization of any size.

Agentic AI production experience is nascent and vaguely described (April 2025 to present, ~2 months at time of resume), falling well short of the role's requirement for demonstrated production multi-agent systems with operational consequences.

No fintech compliance, credit-risk, or regulated-environment ownership — fintech exposure at RIDE Capital and Optilyz was feature development, not infrastructure with regulatory or financial-risk accountability.
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically strong individual contributor with genuine distributed-systems experience and early-stage AI-agent work, but the role is an executive CTO position requiring demonstrated org-building, multi-agent production ownership, and regulated-fintech accountability — none of which appear in his history. The gap is not a matter of degree; the core competencies the role is hiring for (scaling teams, board communication, production AI with financial consequences) are absent from the resume entirely.
